summary: Matched the Mario Cape item unit by adding the missing two-entry ItemStateTable, correcting one GALE01 sdata2 symbol from a global address-style name to a local @ label, moving an unchanged destroyed callback earlier in the C file for ordering, and flipping itmariocape.c from NonMatching to Matching. The decomp-dev bot reported +816 linked code bytes, +32 matched data bytes, +40 linked data bytes, and one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Added it_803F70F8 ItemStateTable and reordered itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed without changing its body.
Changed it_804DCFB8 to local symbol @151 in .sdata2.

what_changed: Added `ItemStateTable it_803F70F8[]` with two rows: state 0 and state 1 both using `itMariocape_UnkMotion1_Anim` and NULL callbacks.

Moved `itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed` from the end of itmariocape.c to before `it_802B2674`; the function still gets `Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` and calls `ftMr_SpecialS_Reset(ip->owner)` only when `ip->owner != NULL`.
Changed GALE01 symbol `.sdata2:0x804DCFB8` from `it_804DCFB8` with global scope to `@151` with local scope.
Bot report confirmed a new itmariocape `.data` match of +32 bytes, moving that item from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Recovered the missing item state table as real C data instead of leaving the unit dependent on an unmatched data blob.
Used source-order adjustment of an existing function to satisfy object layout while preserving behavior.
Fixed the symbol map scope/name for a float constant, recognizing that a local `@151` label was needed instead of a global `it_804DCFB8` symbol.
Only marked the object Matching after the data definition, function ordering, and symbol-map changes were in place.
decomp_lessons: For item files, `ItemStateTable` placement can be part of the match; adding a small top-level table may be enough to complete `.data` matching.
Do not assume address-style `it_804Dxxxx` sdata2 names are global; local constants may need `@NNN` labels in symbols.txt.
Function body correctness is not always enough: top-level declaration/order in the C file can affect matching and linked layout.
A no-op semantic move of a lifecycle callback can be a valid matching tactic when the original object order differs.
Use the decomp-dev report to distinguish matched data progress from general linked code/data byte changes.
naming_conventions: `it_803F70F8` was used for an address-named item data object when no semantic table name was supplied.
`itMariocape_UnkMotion1_Anim` keeps the item prefix and an `UnkMotion1_Anim` suffix for an as-yet-unspecified animation callback.
`itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed` uses the item name, logic state number, and lifecycle event suffix.
Unknown item functions in this file retained address-style names such as `it_802B2560`, `it_802B2674`, and `it_802B2870`.
Local sdata2 constants may be represented as `@151` rather than global address-style names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Emit a two-entry `ItemStateTable` to account for the reported +32 bytes of matched `.data`.
Move `itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed` earlier in the translation unit to align object ordering without altering generated logic.
Correct `.sdata2:0x804DCFB8` to a local `@151` symbol to align constant labeling/scope.
Flip the configure.py object status from NonMatching to Matching once the unit links and data match.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the provided slice.

The only feedback was the decomp-dev bot report for GALE01, which showed +816 linked code bytes, +32 matched data bytes, +40 linked data bytes, and one new `.data` match for `main/melee/it/items/itmariocape`.
